resposta:
solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def canBeEqual(self, target: List[int], arr: List[int]) -> bool:
        from collections import Counter
        return Counter(target) == Counter(arr)
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses the Counter class from the collections module to count the frequency of each element in both the target and arr lists. It then compares these two counts to determine if the lists are equal. This approach has a high efficiency as it only requires a single pass through the lists and uses a hash-based data structure to store the counts.
